# Pass-window reference fixture (L3 gate)

`iss_passes_window.json` is the independent reference for the P3 pass-finder
(ADR-5 / FR-9). It is generated by `tool/gen_pass_fixtures.py` using
**[Skyfield](https://rhodesmill.org/skyfield/) version 1.54** (numpy 2.4.6).

## Method

For a fixed ISS (ZARYA) TLE (NORAD 25544, epoch ~2024 day 122) and a fixed
observer (Warsaw: 52.2297 N, 21.0122 E, 100 m), over a 3-day window starting at
the TLE epoch, the generator calls:

```python
sat.find_events(observer, t0, t1, altitude_degrees=10.0)
```

which returns rise (0), culminate (1), and set (2) events at a **10 deg**
minimum elevation. For each event the geometric alt/az/range is recorded by
calling `altaz()` **without** temperature/pressure, i.e. **no atmospheric
refraction** - matching the package, which models none (ADR-4 / NG5).

Only fully-bracketed rise -> culminate -> set triples wholly inside the window
are emitted, matching the Dart boundary policy (a pass in progress at either
window edge is skipped).

## Why this is a fair, independent gate

Skyfield and `satellite_observer` both run SGP4, so the propagation is shared;
the ONLY thing under test is the **pass-finding** (event bracketing + refine).
The Dart test (`test/passes/iss_passes_window_test.dart`) feeds the SAME TLE,
observer, window, and 10-deg threshold into `SatelliteObserver.passes`, so any
difference in pass count or event times is a real pass-finder bug, not a frame
or ephemeris difference.

## Regenerating

```sh
python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install skyfield
.venv/bin/python tool/gen_pass_fixtures.py
```

Skyfield is BSD-licensed; credit: Brandon Rhodes, the Skyfield project.
